channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Disrupting intestinal cell pyroptosis-NETs crosstalk reverse mucosal damage and restore Treg/Th17 equilibrium in ulcerative colitis via a calcium pectate microsphere composite hydrogel.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - The crosstalk between dysregulated intestinal epithelial cells (IECs) death and hyperactivated immune cells is crucial to maintain the intestinal barrier integrity in ulcerative colitis (UC), but r...

item.title: Physcion-Loaded, Folic Acid-Conjugated DSPE-PEG Liposomes for Targeted Therapy of Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of drug targeting - Conventional therapeutic agents for ulcerative colitis (UC) exhibit limited efficacy and toxic side effects. Although the natural compound physcion (PHY) demonstrates multi-target anti-inflammatory...

item.title: BIRC3 (Encoding Cellular Inhibitor of Apoptosis Protein 2) Variants Result in Dysregulated Receptor-Interacting Protein Kinase 1 Signaling Leading to Increased Epithelial Cell Death and Are Associated With Monogenic Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Tumor necrosis factor (TNF) is a key driver of intestinal epithelial inflammation. The baculoviral inhibitor of apoptosis protein repeat-containing 3 (BIRC3) gene encodes the cellular inhibitor of ...

item.title: Potential mechanism of Lactiflorin in treating ulcerative colitis via modulation of the PI3K/AKT pathway: a study integrating network analysis, bioinformatics analysis, and experimental evidence.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - This study is aimed at investigating the therapeutic effects and the relevant mechanisms of Lactiflorin in ulcerative colitis (UC) via a combination of various methodologies. The PI3K/AKT pathway i...

item.title: Intravenous Immunoglobulin Reveals a Novel Protective Mechanism: Targeting the GBP5-Driven Pyroptosis Axis in Experimental Colitis.
item.description: Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland) - Background: Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by mucosal barrier disruption and dysregulated immune responses. While Intravenous Immunoglobulin (IVIG) is...

item.title: Selenium nanoparticles alleviate ulcerative colitis by regulating macrophage mitochondrial dynamics.
item.description: Free radical biology & medicine - Ulcerative colitis (UC) progression is closely associated with aberrant macrophage polarization and mitochondrial dysfunction. Here, we explored how biogenic selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) protect ...

item.title: Ulcerative colitis: from molecular pathophysiology to management and the emerging role of natural and pharmacological drug candidates.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, relapsing, immune-mediated inflammatory disease of the colonic mucosa that imposes a substantial and growing global health burden. The pathophysiological basis...

item.title: Sulfasalazine- and Cerium Ion-Loaded Polymersomes with Enhanced RONS Scavenging and Colonic Mucosal Repair Ameliorate Experimental Colitis.
item.description: Biomacromolecules - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a relapsing gastrointestinal disorder depicted by persistent oxidative/nitrosative stress, mucosal barrier disruption, and maladaptive immune activation. Herein,...

item.title: Oridonin ameliorates ulcerative colitis by regulating the PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling pathway to activate autophagy.
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - The present study aimed to investigate the therapeutic effects and underlying mechanism of oridonin (ORI) in ulcerative colitis (UC) using the lipopolysaccharide (LPS)‑induced macrophage inflammato...

item.title: An Attention-Enhanced ViT-HLNN Hybrid Ensemble Framework for Multi-Class Gastrointestinal Disease Classification.
item.description: Scientific reports - Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases such as polyps, esophagitis, and ulcerative colitis pose significant diagnostic challenges due to subtle visual patterns. Early, accurate, and scalable diagnostic too...

item.title: Deep Eutectic Solvent-Based Extraction Optimization, Structural Characterization, and Alleviating Effects of Tremella fuciformis Polysaccharides on Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Foods (Basel, Switzerland) - Tremella fuciformis polysaccharides (TFPS) exhibit anti-inflammatory and gut-microbiota-modulating activities, but conventional extraction methods often show limited efficiency and may affect polys...

item.title: Betulinic Acid Enema Alleviates Colitis by Targeting Vitamin D Receptor to Protect the Intestinal Mucosal Barrier and Modulate Mucosa-Associated Microbiota.
item.description: Phytotherapy research : PTR - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease of the colon with limited effectiveness and safety of current drug therapies. Betulinic acid (BA) is a naturally occurring pentacyclic trit...

item.title: Translational Animal Models in Colitis: From Rodents to Pig and Minipig Platforms.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is a chronic relapsing inflammatory disorder characterized by epithelial barrier dysfunction, immune dy...

item.title: Red Ginseng Ethanolic Extract Alleviates DSS-Induced Colitis in Mice by Suppressing Inflammatory Mediator Production.
item.description: International journal of molecular sciences - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by recurrent intestinal inflammation and mucosal injury. This study evaluated the protective potential of red ginseng e...

item.title: Evaluation of the Intestinal Anti-Inflammatory Property of Spondias mombin L. in an Experimental Animal Model of Colitis.
item.description: Pharmaceutics - Background/Objectives: Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are conditions of the gastrointestinal tract with treatments linked to side effects and relapses. Spondias mombin L. is a species with anti-...

item.title: ANGPT1-GABARAP axis modulates NLRP3 inflammasome-mediated pyroptosis in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Crohn's disease (CD) is characterized by persistent intestinal inflammation, immune dysregulation, and intestinal barrier dysfunction. Inflammasome-mediated pyroptosis is an innate immune mechanism...

item.title: Novel mouse models for perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease reveal therapeutic value of interferon-γ antagonists.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease (PFCD) is a challenging complication with poorly understood pathogenesis and limited treatment options, largely due to the lack of clinically relevant animal mo...

item.title: Integrative Network Toxicology Reveals Potential Molecular Targets Linking Plasticizer Exposure to Inflammatory Gastrointestinal Disorders.
item.description: Genes - Plasticizers, including phthalate esters and phthalate-free alternatives, are widely detected environmental chemicals. Although increasing evidence suggests that plasticizers may disrupt gastrointe...

item.title: Advanced Targeted Curcumin Delivery Using Spatiotemporally Controlled Nanohybrid Polysaccharide-Based Hydrogel for Ulcerative Colitis Therapy.
item.description: Gels (Basel, Switzerland) - In ulcerative colitis (UC), the therapeutic efficacy of nanoparticle (NP)-based drug delivery systems is limited by premature drug release, uptake or degradation of NPs during their passage through...
